scenarios.description: Earnings optimism + continued ceasefire sentiment drives IT/financials higher
scenarios.trigger: TCS/INFY results beat expectations; April 10 talks proceed without incident

scenarios.description: Profit-booking after +3.72% rally; consolidation with earnings-driven volatility
scenarios.trigger: Mixed earnings; April 10 talks neutral; FII flows unchanged

scenarios.description: Profit-taking accelerates; April 10 Islamabad talks signal breakdown risk
scenarios.trigger: Earnings disappoint; Islamabad talks show no progress; FII selling resumes

confidence_reasoning: April 8 relief rally is now priced in. Session April 9 will be dominated by: (1) Profit-booking after +3.72% day (2) TCS/INFY earnings calendar (3) April 10 Islamabad talks uncertainty (4) FII flows. No single catalyst expected. Consolidation is most likely. Confidence is 3⭐ because April 10 talks introduce binary risk (not priced in yet).
key_factors: TCS, INFY, HCLTECH earnings (announcements expected this week)
April 10 Islamabad talks — next geopolitical binary event
FII flows — structurally weak; ceasefire doesn't change long-term flows
Brent crude — watch for stabilization or fresh weakness
Rupee strength — ceasefire removes depreciation fears; may cap rupee rally

sector_watch.watch_points: Q1 margin guidance; US Q1 earnings season; USD strength
Ceasefire is 2-week duration; April 10 talks progress. If deal expands to 4+ weeks, premium extends. If talks fail, premium could reverse.
Brent range $90-95 if ceasefire holds. Watch for OPEC comments or production changes.
Q4 margins on NPA recognition; asset quality; deposit growth

major_holdings_watch.rationale: Defense premium extended (2-week ceasefire), but P012 target of ₹1,050-1,080 may face profit-booking. April 10 talks are key catalyst.
major_holdings_watch.action: Hold for now; consider partial profit-taking if approaches ₹1,050. Avoid chasing new highs.

major_holdings_watch.rationale: Ceasefire removed acute ATF cost panic. P015 thesis is consolidation range. Q4FY26 results (May) will drive next move.
major_holdings_watch.action: Hold; watch for earnings announcement date. If stock approaches ₹4,300, consider selling on bounce.

major_holdings_watch.rationale: P002 (₹160-170 downside) invalidated by ceasefire. Brent <$95 means GRM recovery. P017 (₹200-210 target) is new thesis.
major_holdings_watch.action: Hold for GRM recovery play. April 9 consolidation is normal; GRM uplift will be visible in Q1FY27 guidance refresh.

major_holdings_watch.rationale: P005 thesis (>₹430) intact. Ceasefire benefits cheap coal substitution thesis further. At ₹449.65, stock has buffer.
major_holdings_watch.action: Hold. No action needed. Continue monitoring above ₹430 support.

fii_dii_outlook.fii_flow_forecast: Continued outflows (-₹2,000 to -₹5,000 Cr range) as geopolitical premium eases and global rate hikes continue
fii_dii_outlook.dii_support: Strong domestic buying expected to absorb FII selling, especially on dips below 23,800
fii_dii_outlook.rupee_impact: Ceasefire removes depreciation fears; rupee may strengthen 2-3% vs dollar over 2-4 weeks

risk_events_april_9: US market reaction to ceasefire (overnight data)
Any negative news from Islamabad talks setup
next_critical_event: April 10 Islamabad talks between US, Iran, intermediaries. Outcome will determine: (1) If deal expands beyond 2 weeks → war premium extends further (2) If talks fail → ceasefire may collapse, Nifty risk-off
